name: Links and Tees Golf Club
about: Links and Tees Golf Club is a public facility operated by the Addison Park District, offering a variety of golf-related activities. The venue features a 9-hole golf course, an indoor golf dome, an outdoor driving range, and a miniature golf course known as Putter's Peak. It emphasizes family fun and quality customer service, providing options for casual play, lessons, and event rentals. The facility caters to a wide range of golfers from beginners to those looking for a casual round of golf.
why_golfers_like_it: Variety of facilities including 9-hole course, indoor dome, driving range, and miniature golf
Emphasis on family-friendly atmosphere and quality customer service
Availability of lessons and event rentals for special occasions

faq.question: Do I need my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not specify whether clubs are provided or if you must bring your own.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The website does not have a policy regarding outside food or drink, so it is unclear.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The facility features a 9-hole golf course, so playing 18 holes would involve completing two loops of the course.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Links and Tees prides itself on being family-friendly and offers a miniature golf course called Putter's Peak.
